mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly payroll growth above 150,000 persons signals robust labor market expansion, reducing equity risk premium and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days through improved consumer spending capacity and corporate earnings expectations.
regime_hint: post-recession recovery with labor market normalization
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive 20-day forward returns occur in < 55% of months where PAYEMS growth exceeds 150k persons.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate remains below 0.25 percent, the zero lower bound constraint signals monetary accommodation that suppresses discount rates and increases equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in < 60% of periods where FEDFUNDS < 0.25%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A joint increase in housing starts (HOUST) and retail sales (RSAFS) signals synchronized demand expansion across residential and consumer sectors, triggering positive equity returns over 20 trading days through accelerated GDP growth expectations and corporate revenue forecasts.
regime_hint: synchronized demand recovery across housing and consumption
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in < 58% of instances where both HOUST > 850k and RSAFS > 420bn, or interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth decelerating below 0.5 percent month-over-month signals supply-side weakness that constrains earnings growth and increases equity risk premium, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days as investors reprrice growth expectations downward.
failure_condition: Negative 5-day returns occur in < 52% of months where INDPRO growth < 0.5%, or Rank IC > -0.02 (fails to show negative correlation).

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate rising above 6.5 percent signals labor market deterioration that reduces consumer confidence and discretionary spending, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days through compressed earnings multiples and lower revenue growth.
failure_condition: Negative 20-day returns occur in < 55% of months where UNRATE > 6.5%, or Rank IC > -0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation measured by CPI rising above 2.5 percent annually forces the Federal Reserve toward tightening, which increases discount rates and reduces equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as rate hike expectations build.
failure_condition: Negative 60-day returns occur in < 54% of periods where CPI YoY > 2.5%, or interaction coefficient between CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) accelerating above 2.5 percent annualized signals robust economic expansion that increases corporate earnings and equity risk appetite, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days through multiple expansion and earnings upgrades.
evidence_refs: fact:2014-04-01 GDPC1 at 15985.7 billion chained 2017 dollars
failure_condition: Positive 60-day returns occur in < 62% of quarters where GDPC1 growth > 2.5% annualized, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) declining below 800,000 units annually signals residential demand weakness that portends broader economic slowdown, reducing equity valuations through lower construction employment and reduced consumer wealth effects, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Negative 20-day returns occur in < 53% of months where HOUST < 800k, or the joint effect of HOUST and PAYEMS shows no significant interaction term (p > 0.10) in logistic regression.
